item.title: Terraform Backends
item.description: Overview Define where Terraform State snapshots are stored. Options: Local (default) Remote—e.g. AWS S3, GCP Cloud Storage Terraform Cloud Configuration 1 2 3 4 5 terraform { backend "remote" { organisation = "example" } } Not every argument required in backend config—partial configuration Supply remaining arguments by: File—supply configuration file (name: *.

item.title: Terraform Commands
item.description: Commands -apply—executes the actions proposed in a Terraform plan destroy—destroys all remote objects managed by the config fmt—rewrites config files to canonical Terraform format/style Also flags syntax errors import—imports existing remote objects into the Terraform state init—initializes a working directory containing Terraform config files.

item.title: Terraform Debug Logs
item.description: Enable Debug Logging Set env var TF_LOG to TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN or ERROR Also TF_LOG_CORE (core Terraform debug logs), TF_LOG_PROVIDER (provider logs) TF_LOG_PATH—append logs to a file

item.title: Terraform Provisioners
item.description: Overview Provisioners model actions on the local or remote machines Prepare infrastructure for service Should be used as a last resort For actions not directly represented in Terraform Anti-Patterns Pass Data into VMS Instead: utilize e.

item.title: Terraform Settings
item.description: Terraform Block 1 2 3 terraform { #... } Sub-elements: cloud—configures Terraform Cloud backend—configures the Terraform state backends required_version—constrains the Terraform version Constraints of all child modules must also be satisfied required_providers—specifies providers required by the module Includes address and version constraint

item.title: Terraform
item.description: Exam Topics Infrastructure as Code (IAC) Terraform State Terraform Providers Terraform Backends Terraform Settings Terraform Provisioners Terraform Commands Terraform Debug Logs

item.title: Terraform Providers
item.description: Overview Allow Terraform to interact with cloud providers, SaaS providers and other APIs Must declare which providers will be used in the config So Terraform can install Configuration Belong in root module Child modules receive config from root module Can use expressions in config arguments, but values must be known at apply-time (e.
